F arn P olrrrcal P RACTrcns C oMMrs sroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street o Sacramenro, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
February T1,2002
Trent J. Benedetti, CPA
Benedetti & Associates
Post Office Box 5958
Santa Maria, CA 93456-5958
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-222
Dear Mr. Benedetti:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of two legislative
candidates regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
QUESTIONS
1. Must a candidate file an amended Form 410 if the candidate's Senate district
number changes as a result of redistricting?
2. Are separate existing campaign accounts, one for an Assembly and one for a
Senate race, to be maintained?
3. Must one amend the Form 501, Statement of Intention, if the candidate's
Senate district number changes as a result of redistricting?
4. Must a candidate for an Assembly seat amend his campaign forms if he or she
decides to campaign for the same seat but in a different election?
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
1 8 1 09 - I 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.
@


File No. A-01-222
Page No. 2
See analysis and conclusions below
CONCLUSIONS
FACTS
The current Assembly member for the 33'dDistrict, Abel Maldonado, has filed to
run for Senate District L8 in2002. This was his geographical seat before redistricting.
Your client, the Assembly member, has received contributions and expenditures for this
campaign. Now the 18ft Senate District has changed boundaries and Assemblyman
Maldonado is in the 15th Senate District. Your clGnt is essentially running two
campaigns - re-election to the 33'd Assembly District and electionin2OO4 in the 15ft
Senate District.
Also, another client, Sam Blakeslee, has filed to run for the 33'd Assembly District
in 2002 and has a bank account with contribution and expenditure activity. If the above
Assembly member stays on untrl2004, Mr. Blakeslee will put off his Assembly
campaign :unt1|2004, as well. You ask what forms/amendments he would need to file in
this situation.
ANALYSIS/CONCLUSIONS
Ouestion #1. Do you file an amended Form 410 to reflect the 15ft Senate District
and the 2004 electron date?
The Form 410, or Statement of Organization, is required to be filed by every
committee under section 82013 within 10 days of becoming a committee. ($ 84101.)
Among the information required on the statement is identification of the elective office
sought. Whenever there is a change in any of the information required to be disclosed,
the filer must file an amended Form 410, reflecting the given change. ($ 84103.) Thus,
the Form 410 must be amended to reflect change from the 18n to the 15ft Senate District.
Question #2. Must separate bank accounts be kept for the Assembly and Senate
campaigns?
Your client currently is the Assembly member for the 33'd Assembly District, and
has filed to run for the Senate seat in the now-l5thSenate District. Upon filing a
statement of intention to run for a given office, a candidate must establish a single bank
account for all campaign activities related to that office. (S 85201.) A candidate must
establish a separate controlled committee for each specific offrce for which a statement of
intention is filed. (Reg 18521.) Accordingly, separate bank accounts must be kept for
the Assembly and Senate campaigns.


File No. A-01-222
Page No. 3
Ouestion #3: Must an amended Form 501 be filed?
A candidate must file a Statement of Intention (Form 501) prior to soliciting or
receiving contributions or loans. ($ 85200.) This statement, among other things,
indicates the office which is being sough't. Because your client now is a candidate for the
15tr Senate District, an amended Form 501 must be filed to reflect this fact. (Reg. I 8427,
subd. (b).) A district number change due to reapportionment does not change the office
sought by the candidate for purposes of section 85200. (LoclEer Advice Letter, A-89-
074 ) Thus, under these unique facts any funds deposited in the campaign account may
be used for the candidate's campaign for the new district and no restrictions or new
reporting requirements are imposed upon the candidate. (Id.)2
Ouestion #4. What amendments must Mr. Blakeslee file?
In your question, you pose the scenario that the current Assembly officeholder
stays in office untll2004. In that event, you ask, what must be done with Mr. Blakeslee's
bank account and other forms if he decides to run not for the 2002 Assembly seat, but for
the2004 Assembly race instead. Mr. Blakeslee may file an amended Form 410 and Form
501 to reflect that he is now a candidate for the 33'd Assembly District seat in 200a. ($$
84103 and 85200; Reg. 18427, subd. (b).)
If you have any other questions regarding this matteq please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
By: C. Scott
Counsel, Legal Division
CSr.jg
I.\Adviceltrs\01-222
' The Commission is currently considering regulations conceming carryover of funds and
redesignation that may inpact this analysis in the future.

4. Must a candidate for an Assembly seat amend his campaign forms if he or she
decides to campaign for the same seat but in a different election?
